intent_satisfaction.original intent: agent-created-aids-lifecycle first child; storage boundaries should exist before manifest, safety, discovery, and promotion work.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Agent-created aid storage and ownership boundaries are queryable, documented, and guarded against ad hoc structured metadata.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): .agentic-workspace/docs/agent-aids-storage.md; defaults/config payloads; ownership ledgers; structured inventory.

durable_residue.learned constraint: Reusable aid storage must stay repo-, agent-, tool-, and language-agnostic, with local-only helpers outside shared authority.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: The candidate root lowers repeated placement decisions but must remain outside ordinary startup and route structured metadata to a manifest contract.
durable_residue.canonical owner now: defaults/config payloads, ownership ledgers, structured inventory, and .agentic-workspace/docs/agent-aids-storage.md
durable_residue.promotion trigger: when a candidate aid becomes stable enough to move to a repo-native or package-owned surface

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #517 was only the boundary slice; manifest, safety, discovery, and promotion remain planned children.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: .agentic-workspace/docs/agent-aids-storage.md, defaults/config payloads, ownership ledger entries, and structured inventory gap entries routed to #518.
closure_check.reopen trigger: agents still cannot tell where to place local-only versus checked-in reusable aids without broad search.

signals found: A compact defaults selector made the boundary cheaper to recover than opening docs or scanning config output.
Structured candidate aid metadata would otherwise become ad hoc before the manifest slice.
signals fixed: Routed structured candidate aid files to #518 through the structured-file inventory.

continuation: #518 manifest/schema follows the structured-file inventory gap.
